item.title: 据《金融时报》报道，英伟达重新调整台积电产能配置，因出口管制阻碍其在华销售
item.description: 据《金融时报》周四报道，英伟达已停止生产面向中国市场的芯片，预计美中两国的监管壁垒将继续限制对全球第二大经济体的销售。【原文：Nvidia has stopped production of ​chips intended for the ‌Chinese market, betting that regulatory barriers ​in the U.S. ​and China will continue ⁠to limit sales ​to the world's ​second-largest economy, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.】

item.title: 加密货币法案陷入新僵局，其前景存疑
item.description: 关于加密货币的里程碑式立法谈判陷入新僵局，此前多家银行表示无法支持白宫推动的妥协方案。这一进展令该法案能否在今年通过存疑，并引发特朗普总统的批评，他指责贷款机构试图破坏该法案。【原文：Talks on landmark crypto legislation have hit a new impasse after banks said they could not back a compromise pushed by the White House, a development that cast doubt on whether the bill will pass this year and sparked criticism from President Donald Trump ​who accused lenders of trying to undermine it.】

item.title: 据《The Information》报道，OpenAI年化营收突破250亿美元
item.description: 据《信息》周三报道，人工智能初创公司OpenAI截至上月底的年化收入已突破250亿美元，该消息援引了知情人士的数据。【原文：Artificial intelligence startup OpenAI topped $25 billion in annualized revenue as of ​the end of last month, The Information ‌reported on Wednesday, citing a person familiar with the figure.】

item.title: 随着中东地区航班逐步恢复，航空公司股价反弹
item.description: 随着更多航班从中东起飞，航空股周四出现反弹。此前美国和以色列对伊朗的空袭导致航空公司市值蒸发数十亿美元，此次航班恢复为航空公司带来喘息之机。【原文：Airline shares rebounded on Thursday as more flights took off from the Middle ​East, providing some reprieve for carriers after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran wiped billions of dollars off their market value earlier in the ‌week.】

item.title: 阿里巴巴组建专项小组推动人工智能发展，此前AI部门负责人离职
item.description: 阿里巴巴集团控股有限公司周四宣布，将成立新工作组以加速基础模型研发，此前其Qwen AI部门负责人林俊阳已辞职。【原文：Alibaba Group Holding ‌Ltd said on Thursday it would set up a new task force to accelerate ​foundation model development, following the resignation ​of its Qwen AI division head Lin ⁠Junyang.】

item.title: 亚洲股市大涨，韩国KOSPI指数领涨；随着战争担忧消退，美国国债价格下跌。
item.description: 亚洲股市周四反弹，美国国债价格下跌，表明风险偏好出现初步复苏迹象。此前因中东局势升级，市场风险偏好遭受重创。【原文：Asian shares rallied on Thursday with a decline in U.S. Treasuries pointing to a tentative recovery in risk ​appetite that has been hammered by the escalating war in the Middle East.】

item.title: 油价上涨，伊朗冲突扩大
item.description: 周四油价上涨，因市场对霍尔木兹海峡长期关闭的担忧加剧。美伊战争阻断了中东关键的石油和天然气供应，同时生产设施的限制也导致产量下降。【原文：Oil prices rose on Thursday amid growing concern over the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz, as the U.S.-Iran war ​chokes off vital Middle East oil and gas flows while production facilities limit ‌output.】

item.title: 中国誓言加速技术自主，大力推进人工智能发展
item.description: 中国周四承诺将加快实现技术自主的步伐，致力于扶持半导体、航空航天和人工智能等尚处发展初期的国内产业。【原文：China vowed on Thursday to accelerate efforts to achieve greater technological ​reliance, pledging to boost still young domestic industries such ‌as semiconductors, aerospace and artificial intelligence.】

item.title: 澳大利亚、加拿大签署关键矿产新协议
item.description: 加拿大总理马克·卡尼周四表示，澳大利亚与加拿大签署了一系列关于关键矿产的新协议，澳大利亚将参与七国集团关键矿产联盟的合作。【原文：Australia and Canada on Thursday signed a series of new agreements ‌on critical minerals, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said, which will involve Australia collaborating with the G7 critical minerals alliance.】

item.title: 韩国芯片业担忧伊朗危机影响中东数据中心计划
item.description: 韩国执政党议员金永培周四表示，韩国芯片行业担忧伊朗危机若长期持续，可能阻碍大型科技公司在中东建设人工智能数据中心的计划，从而抑制强劲的芯片需求。【原文：South Korea's ​chip industry has raised concerns that ‌a prolonged Iranian crisis may deal a setback to plans by big ​tech firms to build ​AI data centres in ⁠the Middle East, thus ​weighing on strong chip demand, ​ruling party lawmaker Kim Young-bae said on Thursday.】

item.title: 美国议员对英特尔测试中国关联企业制造的工具表示担忧
item.description: 由民主党参议员伊丽莎白·沃伦和共和党参议员汤姆·科顿领导的美国两党议员小组周三对英特尔测试ACM Research公司制造的芯片制造设备提出国家安全担忧，路透社率先报道了这一消息。【原文：A bipartisan group of U.S. lawmakers led by Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren and Republican Tom Cotton on Wednesday raised national security concerns over Intel's testing of chipmaking tools made ​by ACM Research, as first reported by Reuters.】

item.title: 美元涨势暂歇，货币市场小幅波动
item.description: 周四美元终止了其迅猛涨势，为饱受打击的欧元带来喘息之机。投资者仍抱有脆弱的预期，认为中东战争可能不会持续到最初预期的那么久。【原文：The dollar halted its blistering rally on Thursday, providing some reprieve to the battered euro as investors clung on to fragile assumptions that the war in the Middle East may not last as long ​as initially expected.】

item.title: Robinhood推出全新白金信用卡，瞄准高净值客户群体
item.description: 罗宾汉周三推出面向高收入客户的新信用卡，试图在美国运通和摩根大通等行业巨头主导的市场中站稳脚跟。【原文：Robinhood launched a new credit card for high-income customers on Wednesday, in an attempt to secure a ​foothold in an industry dominated by heavyweights such as American ‌Express and JPMorgan Chase .】

item.title: 强生公司推出面向消费者直销的网站
item.description: 强生公司已推出一个网站，直接向美国无保险或自费购药的患者销售部分药品。【原文：Johnson & Johnson has launched a website to sell some of its drugs directly ​to U.S. patients who either don't have ‌insurance or pay for their drugs out of pocket.】

item.title: 据《华尔街日报》报道，摩根士丹利在所有部门裁员2500人。
item.description: 据《华尔街日报》周三报道，投资银行巨头摩根士丹利已裁减2500名员工，涉及所有业务部门。该报道援引知情人士消息称。【原文：Investment banking giant Morgan Stanley has laid off 2,500 employees across all divisions, the Wall Street Journal reported on ​Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: 交易日暂歇，但能持续多久？
item.description: 周三，美国和欧洲股市大幅上涨，市场对中东冲突可能很快平息的预期提振了投资情绪，同时油价和能源市场的企稳也为其他受挫资产的反弹奠定了基础。【原文：U.S. and European stock markets registered solid gains on Wednesday, on hopes that conflict in the Middle East might soon cool, while stabilization in oil and energy markets also helped lay the ground for a rebound in other beaten-down assets.】

item.title: Palantir面临挑战，需将Anthropic从五角大楼的人工智能软件中移除
item.description: 在人工智能实验室与五角大楼就安全防护措施发生争执后，Palantir成为最新一家面临与Anthropic解除合作关系的艰难任务的公司，这引发了人们对关键军事软件平台的质疑。【原文：Palantir is the latest company to face the painful task ​of unwinding from Anthropic in the wake of the AI lab's dispute with the Pentagon over safety guardrails, raising ‌questions about a key military software platform.】

item.title: 亚马逊再度裁员，此次涉及机器人部门
item.description: 亚马逊周二证实已裁撤机器人部门员工，至少100个白领岗位受影响，两名知情人士向路透社透露。【原文：Amazon on Tuesday confirmed it laid off staff across its robotics unit, with at least ​100 white-collar jobs affected, two people familiar with ‌the matter told Reuters.】

item.title: 美国鹰牌预测销售额增长将超预期，得益于富裕年轻消费者的需求
item.description: 美国鹰牌服饰公司周三预测全年销售额将超出华尔街预期，其服装及配饰产品凭借营销驱动的消费需求，获得了富裕消费者的青睐。【原文：American Eagle Outfitters forecast annual sales above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, backed ​by marketing-driven demand for its apparel and accessories from affluent shoppers.】

item.title: 博通营收超预期，人工智能推动定制芯片需求
item.description: 芯片设计公司博通周三预测第二季度营收将超出华尔街预期，这表明用于人工智能应用的数据中心对先进芯片的需求旺盛。【原文：Chip designer Broadcom forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, a sign ​of strong demand for advanced chips used in data centers powering artificial ‌intelligence applications.】

item.title: Okta预测在经济不确定性背景下，公司将迎来上市以来最缓慢的营收增长
item.description: Okta周三公布的季度营收预期低于华尔街预期，预计将出现2017年首次公开募股（IPO）以来的首次个位数增长，因经济不确定性抑制了企业技术支出。【原文：Okta forecast first-quarter revenue below Wall Street expectations on Wednesday, projecting single-digit growth for the first ​time since its 2017 IPO as economic uncertainty ‌weighs on enterprise technology spending.】

item.title: 法官命令特朗普政府对进入美国的商品完成关税评估程序，此前相关关税已被裁定无效。
item.description: 据法庭文件显示，美国贸易法庭一名法官周三下令政府完成货物入境手续，无需征收上月被美国最高法院裁定无效的关税。【原文：A U.S. trade ‌court judge on Wednesday ordered the government ​to finalize ​goods entries into the ⁠United States without ​assessing tariffs that ​were struck down by the U.S. Supreme Court ​last month, according ​to a court filing.】

item.title: 中国全国人大将审议批准经济增长与政策规划，美中竞争日益加剧
item.description: 中国年度人大会议于周四开幕，预计北京将公布其升级产业基础、缩小与美国的科技差距以及重申提振消费需求的雄心。【原文：China’s annual parliament meeting opens on Thursday, with Beijing expected to unveil ambitions to upgrade its industrial base, close the technology gap with the ​U.S. and renew its pledge to shore up consumer demand.】

item.title: 花旗将铝0-3个月合约目标价上调至每吨3600美元
item.description: 花旗将伦敦金属交易所铝0-3个月合约价格目标上调至每吨3600美元，此前目标为3400美元/吨，并表示在伊朗战争爆发后供应中断及不可抗力声明的背景下，价格在乐观情景下可能攀升至4000美元/吨。【原文：Citi has raised its LME aluminium 0–3 month price ​target to $3,600 a metric ton, up from $3,400/t, and said prices could climb to $4,000/t ‌in a bull-case scenario, citing supply disruptions and force majeure declarations after war broke out in Iran.】

item.title: 服装科技初创公司CaaStle创始人承认3亿美元欺诈案罪名成立
item.description: 联邦检察官称，破产服装科技初创公司CaaStle的创始人周三承认，其通过欺诈手段骗取投资者逾3亿美元。【原文：The once-prominent entrepreneur of bankrupt clothing technology startup CaaStle pleaded guilty on Wednesday in connection with ​her scheme to defraud investors out of more ‌than $300 million, federal prosecutors said.】

item.title: 美国柴油价格突破每加仑4美元，中东冲突升级推高燃料成本
item.description: 周三，美国零售柴油均价时隔近两年首次突破每加仑4美元大关，且随着美以与伊朗的冲突持续升级，本周晚些时候油价预计将进一步攀升，这可能导致从食品到家具等各类商品价格大幅上涨，令消费者面临"价格标签冲击"。【原文：Average U.S. retail diesel prices surpassed $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly two years on ​Wednesday and are set to rise further later in the week as the U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran dragged on, threatening ‌to hit consumers with sticker shocks on everything from food to furniture.】

item.title: 美洲重质原油价格触及多年高点，伊朗冲突扰乱石油市场
item.description: 周三，美洲产重质原油价格飙升至多年高位，因美以对伊朗的袭击阻碍了中东产同类原油的出口。【原文：Prices for heavy crude produced in the Americas surged to multi-year highs on Wednesday, as the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran stymied exports of similar oil ​produced in the Middle East.】

item.title: 美国邮政局正招聘重组顾问，因其资金可能在2027年耗尽
item.description: 美国邮政局长戴维·施泰纳在接受路透社采访时表示，美国邮政局正聘请重组顾问以应对日益加剧的财务困境。【原文：The U.S. Postal Service is hiring restructuring advisers to help address its mounting financial troubles, ​Postmaster General David Steiner told Reuters in an interview.】

item.title: 美联储报告显示经济前景向好，就业形势稳定
item.description: 美联储周三在一份报告中称，近期美国经济活动略有回升，物价持续上涨，就业水平保持稳定。这份报告可能让央行决策者在为两周后的下次政策会议做准备时，对通胀走势感到困惑。【原文：U.S. economic activity rose a bit, prices ​continued to increase and employment levels were stable in recent weeks, the Federal Reserve said on Wednesday ‌in a report that may leave central bank policymakers puzzling about the path of inflation as they prepare for their next policy meeting in two weeks.】

item.title: 地中海航运公司宣布对部分航线征收紧急燃油附加费
item.description: 地中海航运公司周三表示，将对部分航线征收紧急燃油附加费，该措施将于3月16日生效。【原文：MSC said on Wednesday it was imposing ​an emergency fuel surcharge ‌on some trade routes, effective March 16.】
